name: Pasta with Chinese Tahini Sauce
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------CHINESE TAHINI SAUCE----------------------------
2 tablespoons Tahini (sesame butter)
2 teaspoons Chili Paste with garlic (hot)
1 tablespoon Rice Vinegar
1 teaspoon Minced Gingerroot
1 tablespoon Soy sauce
2 tablespoons Vegetable stock or water
1 tablespoon (preferably toasted) Sesame Oil
1 dash Freshly ground black pepper
preferably buckwheat noodles or Chinese wheat noodles
GARNISHES: scallion curls, fresh coriander sprigs, toasted sesame seeds, or chopped dry-roasted unsalted peanuts, optional.
NOTE:
Chinese wheat noodles are compacted into cubes. To cook, break the cubes and drop them into boiling water. When the water returns to a boil, cook for 3 minutes and drain. They may be served as is or patted dry and sautéed in oil until lightly browned.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil, cook pasta until al dente. While the pasta is cooking, steam the peas. In a large bowl, combine the remaining ingredients.
When the pasta is done, drain well. Toss the dressing with the pasta; add the peas and toss again. Top with garnish.
VARIATIONS: - Sauté 6 ounces chopped spinach in 2 tablespoons safflower oil until limp; mushrooms, blanched edamame pods, sweet red peppers, steamed broccoli, or steamed sliced carrots. If you add several ingredients, the amount of dressing may need to be increased.
